violations.text: Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (knives) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC washed/sanitized. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/23/2025
Inspector observed live cat in establishment. New Violation. Correct By: 12/23/2025

The doors to the outside, located in the (front and beverage walk-in), are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/15/2025
The floors in the walk-in freezer and beverage walk-in areas are in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 12/23/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (knives) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC washed/sanitized. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/11/2025

Various deli meat and cheese were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/01/2025
Various commercially prepared items (dried beans, French fries, and garlic bread) were observed without labels. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/01/2025

The door to the outside, located in the front and walk-in beverage cooler, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 12/15/2025
Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the produce area. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/01/2025

Inspector observed dumpster in poor repair (missing top lids). Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. New Violation. Correct By: 12/31/2025

The rafters in the meat walk-in cooler have an accumulation dark and white debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/01/2025
The walk-in freezer and beverage cooler floors were observed in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 12/31/2025
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 12/01/2025

An open employees' beverage container was observed stored in a produce preparation area. Employees shall not drink from open beverage containers in order to prevent contamination of their hands, the container, and exposed food. PIC removed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/01/2025
Inspector observed various pastries from an unapproved source. PIC discarded. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/01/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicers, knives, bandsaw, ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC took items out of service to be washed and sanitized. Ice was disposed of by PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/01/2025

Inspector observed eggs and cheese held between 48-53 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Based on time, PIC placed items in walk-in cooler for active cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/01/2025

Deli meat and cheese were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 12/01/2025

The establishment is lacking a thermometer to test food temperatures. Food temperature measuring devices shall be provided and be readily accessible for checking cooking, cooling, reheating, and hot/cold holding temperatures of time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. New Violation. Correct By: 12/01/2025
Various commercially prepared items (dried beans, frozen French fries, and frozen garlic bread) were observed without labels. New Violation. Correct By: 12/01/2025

The doors to the outside, located in the front and beverage walk-in cooler, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 12/15/2025
Cat was observed in establishment. PIC to remove cat from facility. New Violation. Correct By: 12/01/2025
Various food items were observed stored on the floor in the produce and walk-in cooler/freezer. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 12/01/2025

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the meat/produce cutting area. New Violation. Correct By: 12/01/2025

The cutting boards in the produce and meat cutting areas are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 12/01/2025
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 12/01/2025

The following non-food contact surfaces of an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: - retail coolers, - meat/deli display cooler, - retail shelves. New Violation. Correct By: 12/01/2025

Inspector observed dumpster in poor repair (missing top lids). Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. New Violation. Correct By: 12/31/2025

The rafters in the meat walk-in cooler has an accumulation of dark and white debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 12/01/2025
The floors in the walk-in freezer and beverage walk-in cooler are in poor repair (holes). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 12/31/2025

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 12/01/2025

Deli meat and cheese were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 07/02/2024

Meat case is lacking a consumer advisory. A written consumer advisory (on the menu, table tent, or placard) is not provided to the consumer informing them of the significantly increased risk associated with certain vulnerable consumers eating such food in a raw or undercooked form. New Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2024
Live cat observed within facility. Live animals may not be allowed on the premises of a food establishment. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/02/2024
Bottled beverages were observed stored in ice used for food and drinks. Ice was voluntarily disposed of during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/02/2024
Food and drinks were observed stored on the floor throughout establishment. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 07/02/2024
Faucet located at 3 bay sink area was not in good repair, observed leak. New Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2024

The cutting boards in the meat and produce area are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the walk-in curtains have an accumulation of black residue and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2024

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2024
